stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 3 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 20 observations with mean 28.2 % (within-trial site SD = 9.1). trial site B has 10 observations with mean 48.1 % (within-trial site SD = 5.5). trial site C has 10 observations with mean 59.1 % (within-trial site SD = 9.8). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 7.7 and 19.2 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site C: 58.1 % (shrunk from raw 59.1). Range [56.6, 58.6] as between-trial site SD varies from 7.7 to 19.2. Pooled mean: 40.9.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 40.9 % (weighted average of 3 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=12.8: B = 0.945.
Interval [56.6, 58.6] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (59.1) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (40.9) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
